Germination: Keep soil moist but not flooded.
Vegetative: Maintain water level at 2-3 cm. Apply Urea if needed.
Flowering: Critical water stage. Prevent draining.
Harvesting: Drain water 10 days before harvest.

Germination: Plant in a deep pit with proper drainage.
Vegetative: Water regularly during dry spells. Apply organic manure.
Flowering: Ensure adequate moisture to prevent button shedding.
Harvesting: Harvest mature nuts every 45 days.

Germination: Plant budded stumps in well-drained pits.
Vegetative: Maintain weed-free base. Apply NPK mixed fertilizers.
Flowering: Protect from abnormal leaf fall diseases.
Harvesting: Commence tapping when girth reaches 50cm.

Germination: Provide constant shade and moisture.
Vegetative: Mulch the plant base. Apply split doses of fertilizers.
Flowering: Ensure bee pollination. Maintain overhead shade.
Harvesting: Harvest capsules manually at 30-day intervals.

Germination: Maintain nursery shade. Water cautiously to avoid root rot.
Vegetative: Prune plants for proper framework. Apply fertilizer.
Flowering: Blossom showers are critical. Control berry borer.
Harvesting: Hand-pick ripe berries selectively.

Germination: Raise cuttings in shaded nursery bags.
Vegetative: Pruning and tipping is essential for canopy formation.
Flowering: Maintain plucking tables. Remove flowering shoots.
Harvesting: Pluck two leaves and a bud regularly.

Germination: Plant grafts in pits filled with topsoil.
Vegetative: Prune low-hanging branches. Monitor for tea mosquito bug.
Flowering: Spray against pests during flushing and flowering.
Harvesting: Collect fallen mature nuts and sun dry.

Germination: Ensure sucker has good root system in well-manured pit.
Vegetative: Apply Potassium-rich fertilizer. Remove weeds and desucker.
Flowering: Propping may be required for support.
Harvesting: Harvest when fingers satisfy angularity and turn light green.

Germination: Plant setts in furrows and maintain optimal moisture.
Vegetative: Earthing up is required to prevent lodging. High nitrogen need.
Flowering: Withhold massive irrigation to aid sugar accumulation.
Harvesting: Harvest close to the ground when stalks turn yellowish.

Germination: Plant seedlings under shade with continuous watering.
Vegetative: Apply organic compost. Control spindle bug.
Flowering: Protect immature nuts from fungal diseases (Koleroga).
Harvesting: Harvest ripe bunches possessing yellow-orange color.

Germination: Tie growing vines to the standard trees. Provide shade.
Vegetative: Apply cow dung and NPK. Control quick wilt disease.
Flowering: Ensure good drainage during monsoons.
Harvesting: Pluck spikes when one or two berries turn red.

Germination: Plant under shade trees (like Arecanut or Coconut).
Vegetative: Formative pruning is crucial for canopy structure.
Flowering: Ensure adequate moisture. Monitor for pod rot.
Harvesting: Harvest fully mature yellow/orange pods carefully.

Germination: Plant cuttings vertically or slanted in mounds.
Vegetative: Weed regularly. Apply potassium to support tuber growth.
Flowering: Ensure adequate drainage. Protect against mosaic virus.
Harvesting: Harvest by uprooting the plant when basal leaves yellow.

Germination: Plant rhizome bits with buds facing up. Mulch heavily.
Vegetative: Keep soil consistently moist but never waterlogged.
Flowering: Apply organic manure. Weed carefully around shoots.
Harvesting: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and start drying.

Germination: Plant healthy rhizomes in raised beds. Apply thick mulch.
Vegetative: Control shoot borer pest. Apply fertilizer split doses.
Flowering: Keep field weed-free to avoid nutrient competition.
Harvesting: Harvest when the above-ground stem dries up completely.

Germination: Protect seedlings from direct sunlight.
Vegetative: Apply slow-release fertilizers. Deep watering during dry seasons.
Flowering: Monitor buds carefully. Prevent fungal diseases on foliage.
Harvesting: Hand-pick unopened flower buds when they turn pinkish.

Germination: Maintain strict shade for young grafts.
Vegetative: Pruning helps establish a strong framework.
Flowering: Ensure appropriate ratio of male to female trees if seeds used.
Harvesting: Harvest fruits when they split open revealing the red mace.

Germination: Plant seedlings in well-drained pits.
Vegetative: Coppice trees at 2 years to encourage multiple shoots.
Flowering: Peel bark when the sap flows freely (after rains).
Harvesting: Harvest straight shoots and peel the bark for drying.

Germination: Plant cuttings and train vines on support trees.
Vegetative: Loop vines to encourage flowering. Provide 50% shade.
Flowering: Hand-pollination is strictly required early morning.
Harvesting: Harvest beans when the tips begin to turn yellow.

Germination: Protect young grafts from direct sun and wind.
Vegetative: Prune center to allow light. Apply fertilizer post-harvest.
Flowering: Withhold water to induce flowering. Protect against hoppers.
Harvesting: Pluck fruits with stalks intact when fully mature.

Germination: Plant in well-drained soil on mounds.
Vegetative: High potassium requirement. Do not let water stagnate.
Flowering: Remove virus-affected plants immediately.
Harvesting: Pluck fruits when a yellow streak appears on the skin.

Germination: Plant robust suckers. Apply basal dose of fertilizer.
Vegetative: Keep beds weed-free. Maintain soil moisture.
Flowering: Apply growth regulators to synchronize flowering if needed.
Harvesting: Harvest when 25% of the fruit turns yellow-orange.

Germination: Ensure proper watering and seed placement.
Vegetative: Monitor for pests and weeds. Apply fertilizer.
Flowering: Ensure adequate irrigation during flowering.
Harvesting: Check maturity signs before harvest.
